audit_items.proof_trace: The primary LDAP authentication entry point `lutil_passwd` guards both `passwd` and `cred` for NULL and zero-length at L274-278 before any dereference, and all downstream check/hash functions individually null-check every `ber_memalloc` result. Public API functions (`lutil_passwd_hash`, `lutil_passwd_add`, `lutil_passwd_generate`) lack defensive null checks on their parameters, but every slapd caller in the LDAP path supplies non-NULL arguments: `passwd_extop` uses a sentinel-terminated loop (`for(i=0; hashes[i]; i++)`) so `hashes[i]` is never NULL at point of call, and `slap_passwd_hash` falls back to the compile-time literal `defhash[0]` guaranteeing a non-NULL scheme string.
